title: Domeykoa amplexicaulis
taxonomy: Domeykoa amplexicaulis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is native to South America and is a member of the genus Domeykoa. The plant is further classified as a member of the tribe Bupleureae, a group of plants commonly known as the buttercup tribe. The family Apiaceae, to which it belongs, is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. Domeykoa amplexicaulis is a relatively rare and understudied species within this family.

intro: Domeykoa amplexicaulis, also known as Guanaco or Guayacán, is a small shrub native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Domeykoa amplexicaulis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
